points: Carbon forms stable covalent bonds by sharing four valence electrons with other atoms to achieve a noble gas configuration.
The element exists in distinct structural allotropes including rigid diamond, slippery conductive graphite, and geodesic fullerenes.
Carbon displays catenation and tetravalency, enabling the formation of vast numbers of stable chains, branches, and rings.
Homologous series consist of compounds with the same functional group where successive members differ by a -CH₂- unit.
Carbon compounds undergo combustion, oxidation, addition, and substitution reactions depending on their saturation levels.
Ethanol and ethanoic acid are commercially crucial compounds used as solvents, fuels, and in the production of esters and soaps.
Soaps function by forming micelles that entrap oily dirt using hydrophilic heads and hydrophobic tails.
Detergents remain effective cleaners in hard water as they do not form insoluble scum with calcium or magnesium ions.
